comment: Validated metrics from Grafana dashboards (rabbitmq_dashboard.json). Metrics collected from RabbitMQ exporter and chaos-toolkit instrumentation.

rate(rabbitmq_messages_published_counter.description: Message publish rate per second (measures message production throughput)

rate(rabbitmq_messages_delivered_counter.description: Message delivery rate per second (consumer consumption throughput)

rabbitmq_queue_depth.description: Queue depth - number of messages pending delivery (0 means queues are empty)

rate(rabbitmq_connection_errors_counter_.description: Connection error rate (should be minimal in healthy system)

rabbitmq_connections_total.description: Total active RabbitMQ connections (producers + consumers + management)

extended_metrics.description: Extended metrics that may be available from RabbitMQ exporter (optional, for enhanced monitoring)

available_metrics.name: rabbitmq_queue_messages_unacknowledged_total
available_metrics.description: Messages sent but not yet acknowledged

notes.publish_deliver_ratio: In healthy system, publish rate ≈ delivery rate. Divergence indicates consumer slowness or queue backlog.
notes.queue_depth_explanation: Queue depth > 100 messages indicates consumers are slower than producers. >1000 messages is critical.
notes.connection_management: Each producer/consumer creates at least one connection. Monitor connections as proxy for client load.
notes.message_flood_test: During message-flood chaos test, expect publish rate to spike and queue_depth to increase significantly.
notes.queue_saturation_test: During queue-saturation test, expect queue_depth to grow and delivery latency to increase.
notes.slow_consumer_test: During slow-consumer test, expect queue_depth to increase while publish rate stays normal.
notes.connection_exhaustion_test: During connection-exhaustion test, expect connection_errors to increase and new connections to fail.
notes.channel_count_note: Each connection can have multiple channels. Monitor channel_total for resource usage patterns.
notes.memory_pressure: When rabbitmq_memory_used approaches rabbitmq_memory_limit, broker may pause publishers to prevent OOM.
notes.all_metrics_validated: All metrics in this file are validated against rabbitmq_dashboard.json Grafana dashboard
